data.title: The earnings tightrope: Will Mag 7 bulls pivot to bottom fishing?
data.description:
As earnings season ramps up, Microsoft (MSFT) stands as the weakest “Magnificent Seven” performer, down about 13.6% year to date, followed by Tesla (TSLA) off roughly 12.7%, while Nvidia (NVDA), Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) have posted solid gains.

The S&P 500 not only broke all-time highs propped up by the Mag 7, but also saw an unprecedented bounce back from its wartime low in March, sliding 9% from its January high before recovering 12% from its late March low.

"We expect this earnings season to be a mixed bag, and investors, not unlike in Q1, will still be harder to please," SA investing group leader Tech Contrarians said. "While we expect investors to be quicker to take profit during earnings on a slip-up post-rally, we are simultaneously expecting to see a lot of bottom fishing."

 

 According to FactSet, the S&P 500’s blended earnings growth rate for the quarter is expected to be 13.2% year-over-year.

Because technology is a major earnings driver and a large share of the S&P 500, the Mag 7 remains in focus this earnings season, with AI spending from Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Meta projected to exceed $650B this year.

Tesla (TSLA) reports Q1 2026 earnings on April 22 after market close, while Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN) and Meta (META) are scheduled for April 29, with Apple (AAPL) following on April 30.

Below are the market’s expectations for each of the Magnificent Seven stocks:

  • Tesla (TSLA) - Expected Q4 revenue of $22.35B, EPS of $0.36.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) - FQ3 expected EPS of $4.07, revenue of $81.39B.
  • Meta Platforms (META) - Q1 expected EPS of $6.74, revenue of $55.56B.
  • Apple (AAPL) - FQ2 expected GAAP EPS of $1.95, revenue of $109.59B.
  • Alphabet (GOOGL) - Expected EPS of $2.63, revenue of $106.99B.
  • Amazon (AMZN) - Expected EPS of $1.65, revenue of $177.17B.
  • Nvidia (NVDA) - May 20: EPS of $1.77, revenue of $78.79B.

Tesla (TSLA): The problem investors face today is that the EV pioneer now faces an equally demanding SpaceX (SPACE) as the rocket company gears up for an IPO, likely this year. Musk’s dual focus worked when both companies were smaller and competition was way less intense. Now, however, "this distraction is starting to bite," one SA analyst said. "While the EV story has been one with a durable moat, the stock is too reliant on the robotics story heading into Q1 earnings later this week."

Microsoft (MSFT): Morgan Stanley's Keith Weiss, who has an Overweight rating and a $650 price target on Microsoft, said that Azure growth in the 39% range would be “clearly seen” as a positive, notable given he believes it's achievable. While another analyst notes that the company is rated 'Sell' due to overvaluation and a likely 30% downside as it transitions to an AI infrastructure model. "MSFT's aggressive CapEx, projected to reach 33% of revenue by 2026, risks trapping the company in a cycle of high reinvestment and declining margins."

Meta Platforms (META): The social media giant at current levels is a hold for anyone who bought in March and a buy on any pullback toward the $560-580 range that a softer Q1 ad read might create, an SA analyst argues. META stock surged 7% on April 8 after the company launched Muse Spark, its first product to come out of Meta Superintelligence Labs, led by Alexandr Wang. Therefore, "Meta needs to justify its AI investments. Llama 4 has been a disappointment," another adds.

Apple (AAPL): Fresh leadership changes are shifting the company’s strategic focus toward its next phase of growth. John Ternus will assume the role of CEO on September 1, 2026, while Tim Cook transitions to executive chairman. Attention will also be on Q3 FY26 guidance for indications of how Apple plans to navigate rising memory costs. The key question that Ternus "has to address is whether Apple is an AI platform, a hardware innovator, or a cash compounder built on distribution and ecosystem control."

Alphabet (GOOGL): Alphabet, meanwhile, is delivering solid growth, driven by its core and AI businesses, supporting strong shareholder returns. However, heavy capex and stakes in SpaceX and Anthropic bring both upside potential and concentrated risk. "Alphabet had a phenomenal last quarter, but it's also worth noting where the company is headed... We have some concerns around the size of Alphabet's capex, but it's clear the company has figured out where and when it needs to invest, and overall it's a great company," one SA analyst writes.

Amazon (AMZN): The tech giant is aggressively moving to being the main AI and space infrastructure economies enabler, with plenty of bullish catalysts not properly priced in by the market right now, Oakoff Investments said. "I expect the upcoming Q1 print to bring some relief to investors who were concerned about the ROIC the firm would generate from its past initiatives and corporate decisions." Meanwhile, Amazon and Anthropic are deepening ties with a $5B investment and a $100B AWS infrastructure commitment, while investors also watch the impact of Middle East drone strikes.

Nvidia (NVDA): KeyBanc recently said its favorite ideas include Intel, Micron Technology, and Nvidia (NVDA) while highlighting its fourth quarter 2025 quarterly semiconductor cycle analysis. Analysts led by John Vinh said that the semiconductor cycle remains intact as the real cyclical recovery is yet to start. NVDA's vertical integration, software lock-in, and government/robotics exposure drive robust, diversified growth and resilience against macroeconomic cycles, SA analyst Dmytro Lebid said.

data.title: Northern Star Resources reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Northern Star Resources press release (NESRF): Q3  SLTIFR at 0.6 injuries per million hours worked
  • Group underlying free cash flow of A$301 million and net mine cash of A$426 million
  • Gold sold totaled 381 koz at an AISC of A$2,709/oz (US$1,854/oz)
  • FY26 guidance of above 1,500 koz gold sold and AISC of A$2,600–2,800/oz
  • Total growth capital expenditure of A$2,315–2,425 million, with revisions to KCGM Mill Expansion Project and KCGM Mill Operational Readiness capex
  • Total exploration expenditure of approximately A$225 million remains unchanged

data.title: ASM International reports Q1 results
data.description:
• ASM International press release (ASMIY): Q1 Adjusted net earnings in Q1 2026 increased by €54MY/Y to €246M, supported by improved operating margin and a positive currency translation result.
  • Revenue of $863M (+2.8% Y/Y).
  • AI-led demand accelerated further in the quarter, underpinned by strong customer investments in capacity to drive long-term AI infrastructure expansion. 
  • Gross margin came in at 53.3% in Q1 2026, supported by a favorable product- and customer mix.
  • Adjusted operating result margin increased to a quarterly record of 33.1% in Q1 2026, reflecting strong revenue growth and expanded gross margin, and continued cost control. 
  • Outlook: at constant currency, revenue is projected to increase to €980 million +/-5% in Q2 2026. H2 is expected to be stronger than H1.

data.title: Nel ASA GAAP EPS of -NOK 0.08, revenue of NOK 151.84M
data.description:
• Nel ASA press release (NLLSF): Q1 GAAP EPS of -NOK 0.08.
  • Revenue of NOK 151.84M (-13.2% Y/Y).
  • Revenue from contracts with customers in the first quarter 2026 was NOK 148 million, a 5% reduction compared to the first quarter 2025 (Q1 2025: 155).
  • EBITDA in the quarter was NOK -100 million (Q1 2025: -115).
  • Order intake in the quarter amounted to NOK 85 million, a 73% decrease from the corresponding quarter last year (Q1 2025: 312).
  • Order backlog was NOK 1 113 million at the end of the quarter, down 24% from the first quarter of 2025 and down 16% from the previous quarter.
  • Cash balance was NOK 1,443 million at quarter end.

data.title: Paladin Energy reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Paladin Energy press release (PALAF): Q3  Sales volumes of 1.03Mlb U3O8 at an average realised price of US$68.3/lb U3O8, reflecting higher proportion of sales into base-escalated contracts during the quarter
  • Cost of production for the quarter was US$40.3/lb, benefiting from utilisation of the remaining previously mined MG3 stockpile.
  • Production of 1.29Mlb U₃O₈, up 5% from the previous quarter, driven by strong processing plant performance
  • LHM FY2026 production guidance range increased to 4.5 to 4.8Mlb U3O8 as a result of the strong year to date performance.

  • Cash and investments of US$219.5M and an undrawn US$70M Revolving Credit Facility at quarter end.

  • Total Recordable Injury Frequency (TRIF) of 3.0 per million hours worked on a 12-month basis.

data.title: Akzo Nobel N.V. Non-GAAP EPS of €0.89, revenue of €2.39B; updates FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Akzo Nobel N.V. press release (AKZOF): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of €0.89.
  • Revenue of €2.39B (-8.4% Y/Y).
  • Organic sales -1%, revenue -9%, year-on-year on FX translation (-5%) and India divestment (-3%).
  • Adjusted EBITDA at €345 mln, up €24 mln YoY excluding €23 mln FX and €13 mln India divestment (2025: €357 mln).
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin expansion to 14.5% (2025: 13.7%) driven by gross margin expansion.
  • Net cash from operating activities -€86 million, up €26 million YoY (2025: -€112 million).
  • Outlook: Based on current market visibility, including current geopolitical developments, and at prevailing trading conditions, the company expects to deliver €100 million of adjusted EBITDA improvement in constant currencies.
  • As a result, adjusted EBITDA for the full-year 2026 is expected to be at or above €1.47 billion, based on year-end 2025 exchange rates and adjusted for the India divestment.
  • For the mid-term, AkzoNobel aims to expand profitability to deliver an adjusted EBITDA margin of above 16% and a return on investment between 16% and 19%, underpinned by organic growth and industrial excellence.
  • The company expects leverage to be around 2 times net debt/adjusted EBITDA by the end of 2026. In the mid-term, AkzoNobel aims to maintain leverage around 2 times, while remaining committed to an investment grade credit rating. Closing of the Axalta merger, which is subject to shareholder and regulatory approvals, is expected in late 2026 or early 2027.

data.title: Evolution AB GAAP EPS of €1.26, revenue of €513.04M
data.description:
• Evolution AB (publ) press release (EVVTY): Q1 GAAP EPS of €1.26.
  • Revenue of €513.04M (-1.5% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA declined 1.9% to €335.3 million (€342.0), corresponding to a margin of 65.4% (65.6).
  • The Board of Directors has decided not to propose any dividend for the financial year 2025.

data.title: Maze Therapeutics prices $150M equity offering
data.description:
Maze Therapeutics (MAZE) has priced its underwritten registered offering of 5.54M shares of its common stock at a price of $23.50 per share.

In addition, and in lieu of common stock, Maze is offering to certain investors pre-funded warrants to purchase up to 850K shares at $23.499 per pre-funded warrant.

The gross proceeds to Maze from the offering are expected to be $150M. Maze currently intends to use any net proceeds from this offering primarily to advance research and development of its product candidates, as well as for general corporate purposes.

The firm expects that the net proceeds, together with its current cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, will fund operations into 2029 based on its current business plan.

The offering is expected to close on or about April 23, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Leerink Partners is acting as sole underwriter for the proposed offering.

Source: Press Release

data.title: OpenAI to commit $1.5B to new 'DeployCo' venture with PE firms: report
data.description:
Artificial intelligence firm OpenAI (OPENAI) is set to commit up to $1.5B to a new joint ​venture with private equity firms, Financial Times reported on ‌Wednesday.

The ChatGPT maker will initially invest $500M of equity into the joint venture, known internally as ​DeployCo, which is expected to be valued at $10B ​in a funding round set to close in early ⁠May, the report said.

DeployCo, a Delaware-listed LLC, is reportedly ​designed to speed up the adoption of OpenAI's workplace tools ​and is a central part of the company’s push into the enterprise market, according to FT. OpenAI will have super-voting shares in DeployCo, FT added.

DeployCo's ​private equity backers will invest for five years, with ​OpenAI guaranteeing them an annual return of 17.5%, the report added.

Microsoft-backed (MSFT) OpenAI also ‌has ⁠the option to add a further $1B at a later date, FT said, with investors including TPG, Bain Capital, Advent International, Brookfield and Goanna Capital investing another $4B.

data.title: Capital Group raises bet on MicroStrategy with $747M purchase
data.description:
• Capital Group's American Funds Fundamental Investors (ANCFX) acquired 4.32M additional shares of MicroStrategy (MSTR) valued at $747M.
  • The buying lifts American Funds’ total stake to 10.33M shares, with the position now worth about $1.78B.
  • The increased stake by Capital Group underscores growing institutional conviction in MicroStrategy, which has evolved into a leveraged proxy for Bitcoin due to its sizable cryptocurrency holdings.
  • MSTR (MSTR) stock has jumped over 18% over the past week amid massive Bitcoin accumulations. The stock price traded ~3% on Wednesday pre-market hours.
